title: duSraBheja — Personal AI Second Brain & MCP Server
tagline: A personal open-brain that ingests everything I capture and makes it answerable by AI agents through MCP.
summary: The problem isn't capturing information — it's retrieving it when you need it and knowing what you know. duSraBheja runs on a single droplet, captures from Discord and 6+ local sources, classifies and embeds everything, and exposes the result to Claude Code, Codex, and a public chat through MCP and HTTP.
resume_bullets: Built a Discord-fed personal AI second brain with an MCP server, a 6-layer canonical memory model (Evidence → Observation → Episode → Thread → Entity → Synthesis), and a 5-page private dashboard.

Pivoted from a TypeScript/WhatsApp/LangGraph stack to Python/Discord/ARQ on day 10; cut Anthropic + OpenAI months later for NVIDIA NIM free-tier models. Same problem, two rewrites, simpler ops each time.
Hardened the public surface with an explicit fact allowlist, daily refresh, output scrubbing (GitHub URLs, IPs, SSNs), and topic gating so the public chat only answers what I've approved.
demonstrates: Async Python at production scale (FastAPI + ARQ + asyncpg + pgvector).
Agentic orchestration with discipline — retrieval tests, confidence-gated routing, owner-approved publish path.
Self-hosted ops on a single $X droplet — five services, Docker Compose, no platform lock-in.
proof: 92 commits in 23 days during the rebuild.
Two crons running, cognition firing on-demand every 20 merges.
Six capture sources (Discord, Apple Notes, Chrome history, project files, git, life exports).
outcomes: Live in production on a single DigitalOcean droplet running five services (brain-redis, brain-bot, brain-worker, brain-mcp, brain-api).
Powers the public chat at /brain and the Claude Code / Codex tool surface I use daily.
constraints: Free-tier model budget — every chat, vision, and embedding call routes through NIM, so token counts and model names get logged for quota visibility, cost is zero.
Single-droplet ops — no horizontal scale, so the public path was engineered to be cache-friendly and fast.

case_study_sections.title: What I built
case_study_sections.summary: Discord `#inbox` capture → ARQ async pipeline (extract → classify → embed → librarian merge → canonical Note) with a 0.75 confidence gate routing low-confidence items to a review flow. A 6-layer canonical memory model so story is presentation, not storage. A FastMCP server exposing search, ask, capture, context, protocol, story, and website tools to Claude Code and Codex. A 5-page private Atlas dashboard with no on-render LLM calls — every page is one or two indexed SQL queries. A public surface backed by an explicit PublicFactRecord allowlist with daily refresh, owner approval, and output scrubbing for repo URLs, IPs, SSNs, IPv6 addresses.

case_study_sections.title: One sharp decision
case_study_sections.summary: Ten days into a TypeScript/WhatsApp/LangGraph stack I pivoted the whole thing to Python/Discord/ARQ. The TS stack had clean infrastructure — NATS, Temporal, pgvector — but was bleeding memory across 10 child processes and Node's async story wasn't native. The rewrite kept the schema discipline but bought native async/await, SQLAlchemy 2.0, real MCP, simpler ops. Then I cut again — removed Anthropic, OpenAI, Whisper, and moved every model call to NVIDIA NIM through one OpenAI-compatible client. Don't romanticize the previous architecture; if the cost curve is wrong, rewrite.

case_study_sections.title: One real struggle
case_study_sections.summary: Vector search silently returned wrong results for a day. asyncpg's parameter binding for pgvector isn't the same as psycopg2's — queries were running but ranking was nonsense. The fix was small, three commits and about 65 lines. The deeper cost was confidence in retrieval. Recovery was discipline: every retrieval path got a test before being shipped, and the librarian and retriever got project-aware ranking. That habit is how I now ship retrieval changes without anxiety.

case_study_sections.title: Outcome
case_study_sections.summary: Live on a single DigitalOcean droplet — brain-redis, brain-bot, brain-worker, brain-mcp, brain-api. Captures from six+ sources, classifies with Llama 3.1 8B, embeds via nv-embedqa-e5-v5 (1024d), merges via Llama 3.3 70B. Two crons (reminders, daily public refresh). Cognition fires on-demand every 20 merges. The public chat at /brain answers from the same allowlisted snapshot you're reading from.
